lines: QUESTION ID 19057 : series 3 - CFTC / NFA Regulations
Original jurisdiction disciplinary actions taken by the NFA against and NFA member falls to:
If a matter is referred to the regional business conduct committee and formal complaint is issued, the hearing panel will have original jurisdiction to impose penalties.
text:
QUESTION ID 19057 : series 3 - CFTC / NFA Regulations
Original jurisdiction disciplinary actions taken by the NFA against and NFA member falls to:
A. hearing panel
B. national business conduct committee
C. NFA director
D. local business conduct committee
correct Answer: A
your Answer: C
If a matter is referred to the regional business conduct committee and formal complaint is issued, the hearing panel will have original jurisdiction to impose penalties.

lines: QUESTION ID 19012 series 3 - CFTC 1' NFA Regulations
A Commodity pool operator or CPO is an organization which invests money contributed by a group of participants in numerous accounts for the purpose of advising
customers on how to invest in futures.
A Commodity Pool Operator or CPO is an organization which invests money contributed by a group of participants to a single account for the purpose of investing the money
in futures. A CPO does not advise customers on how to invest money in their accounts, but rather manages a single account on a discretionary basis for the benefit of all
text:
QUESTION ID 19012 series 3 - CFTC 1' NFA Regulations
A Commodity pool operator or CPO is an organization which invests money contributed by a group of participants in numerous accounts for the purpose of advising
customers on how to invest in futures.
( True/False)
correct Answer: B
your Answer: A
A Commodity Pool Operator or CPO is an organization which invests money contributed by a group of participants to a single account for the purpose of investing the money
in futures. A CPO does not advise customers on how to invest money in their accounts, but rather manages a single account on a discretionary basis for the benefit of all
participants.

lines: QUESTION ID Ig118 : Series 3 • Futures & Forwords (wrong)
As it relates to the clearinghouse, which of the following is I are true ?
l. The clearinghouse will net the purchases and sales for all transactions executed during the trading session
Il. The clearinghouse will calculate the original margin requirement
Ill. The clearinghouse will settle futures trades no later than 2 business days
IV. The clearinghouse requires members to deposit margin by the opening of trading on the second business day
The statements that the clearinghouse will net the purchases and sales for all transactions executed during the trading session and the clearinghouse will calculate the original margin requirement
are true. The clearinghouse requires all margin deposits to be made by the opening of trading the next business day and all trades in futures contracts settle the next business day.

lines: A delivery notice received by a futures trader which requires the trader to accept delivery of the underlying commodity, is known as:
A delivery notice received by a futures trader which requires the trader to accept delivery of the underlying commodity, is known as a stopped notice. With a stopped notice
the trader may not offset the requirement to accept delivery by selling the contract.
text:
QUESTION ID 19108 : series 3 - Futures & Forwards
A delivery notice received by a futures trader which requires the trader to accept delivery of the underlying commodity, is known as:
A. a firm notice
B. a stopped notice
C. a forced notice
D. a hard notice
correct Answer: B
your Answer: A
A delivery notice received by a futures trader which requires the trader to accept delivery of the underlying commodity, is known as a stopped notice. With a stopped notice
the trader may not offset the requirement to accept delivery by selling the contract.

lines: Family Farms, grows and produces corn in Iowa. Family Farms has entered into a contract with a local user of corn to sell a significant portion of this year's crop to the
local user. Which of the following is not true?
A. Family Farms may more efficiently operate its business
B. The local user has not taken on any performance risk
C. Family Farms has counterparty risk
D. This is a forward contract
All of the choices are true except the local user has taken on performance risk. If Family Farms cannot deliver the agreed upon amount of corn, the local user will have to
obtain it elsewhere and may have to pay a higher price for the corn.
text:
QUESTION ID 19094 : series 3 - Futures & Forwards
Family Farms, grows and produces corn in Iowa. Family Farms has entered into a contract with a local user of corn to sell a significant portion of this year's crop to the
local user. Which of the following is not true?
A. Family Farms may more efficiently operate its business
B. The local user has not taken on any performance risk
C. Family Farms has counterparty risk
D. This is a forward contract
correct Answer: B
your Answer: D
All of the choices are true except the local user has taken on performance risk. If Family Farms cannot deliver the agreed upon amount of corn, the local user will have to
obtain it elsewhere and may have to pay a higher price for the corn.

lines: Five contract delivery months for one commodity have closed limit up. The Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) has expanded the daily price limit for the contract. The
expanded price limit will remain in place for:
If the price limits are expanded by the exchange, the expanded daily price limits will remain in effect for three consecutive trading days.
text:
QUESTION ID 19155 : series3 Futures Pricing
Five contract delivery months for one commodity have closed limit up. The Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) has expanded the daily price limit for the contract. The
expanded price limit will remain in place for:
A. I trading day
B. 2 trading days
C. 3 trading days
D. 5 trading days
correct Answer: C
your Answer: A
If the price limits are expanded by the exchange, the expanded daily price limits will remain in effect for three consecutive trading days.

lines: The minimum price variation per contract is known as a tick and is set by the CFTC
The minimum price variation per contract is known as a tick and is set by the exchange where the contract trades, not by the CFTC.
text:
QUESTION ID 19140 : series3 Futures Pricing
The minimum price variation per contract is known as a tick and is set by the CFTC
( True/False)
correct Answer: B
your Answer: A
The minimum price variation per contract is known as a tick and is set by the exchange where the contract trades, not by the CFTC.

lines: QUESTION ID 19292 : series 3 options and spreads
When an investor sells a put option to open with no other option or futures position in the account, the investor's maximum loss is unlimited.
When an investor sells a put their maximum loss is not unlimited because the price of the futures contract cannot fall below zero. The maximum loss for a put seller equals
the strike price of the put minus the amount of premium they received when they sold the put.
text:
QUESTION ID 19292 : series 3 options and spreads
When an investor sells a put option to open with no other option or futures position in the account, the investor's maximum loss is unlimited.
( True/False)
correct Answer: B
your Answer: A
When an investor sells a put their maximum loss is not unlimited because the price of the futures contract cannot fall below zero. The maximum loss for a put seller equals
the strike price of the put minus the amount of premium they received when they sold the put.

lines: QUESTION ID 19268 series 3 options and spreads
When looking at the put options on March soybean futures, the higher the futures contract price in relation to the strike price of any particular option, the higher the

The higher the price of the futures contract the less in the money or further out of the money a put option will be. Therefore the higher the price of the futures contract the lower the delta of the put option.
text:
QUESTION ID 19268 series 3 options and spreads
When looking at the put options on March soybean futures, the higher the futures contract price in relation to the strike price of any particular option, the higher the
option's delta.
( True/False)
correct Answer: B
your Answer: A
The higher the price of the futures contract the less in the money or further out of the money a put option will be. Therefore the higher the price of the futures contract the lower the delta of the put option.

lines: QUESTION ID 18667 : Series 3 Options and Spreads
You are short 5 December crude at 73.20 and crude has fallen to 69.40. You think that crude will continue to decline but want to protect your profit in case the crude rallies
back. If you purchase 5 December crude 70 calls at .85 and you have to exercise the call at expiration to offset your short futures position, what is your gain ? Each

You made $11,750 on the trade. You have offset your short crude by exercising the calls. Your cost was the $70 strike price plus the premium of .85 or 70.85. You received 73.20 when you sold the futures short so your
gain was 2.35 per barrel or $2,350 per contract X 5 contracts = $11,750 for the entire position.
text:
QUESTION ID 18667 : Series 3 Options and Spreads
You are short 5 December crude at 73.20 and crude has fallen to 69.40. You think that crude will continue to decline but want to protect your profit in case the crude rallies
back. If you purchase 5 December crude 70 calls at .85 and you have to exercise the call at expiration to offset your short futures position, what is your gain ? Each
contract covers 1,000 barrels of crude.
A. $2,350 gain
B. 31,750
C. $2,950 gain
D. $14,750 gain
correct Answer: B
your Answer: D
You made $11,750 on the trade. You have offset your short crude by exercising the calls. Your cost was the $70 strike price plus the premium of .85 or 70.85. You received 73.20 when you sold the futures short so your
gain was 2.35 per barrel or $2,350 per contract X 5 contracts = $11,750 for the entire position.

lines: The open trade equity for a futures position is calculated based on the:
IV. Less interest and carrying charges
The open trade equity is calculated based on the initial margin deposit plus or minus any unrealized profit or loss on the open contract position. There is no debit balance or
or Ioan so no interest or carrying charges apply to the futures contract position.
text:
QUESTION ID 19236 : series 3 - speculation & Hedging
The open trade equity for a futures position is calculated based on the:
l. Initial margin deposit
Il. Less any unrealized loss
Ill. Plus any unrealized gain
IV. Less interest and carrying charges
A. I and Il
B. l, Il and Ill
C. I and IV
D. l, Il, Ill, IV
» correct Answer: B
» your Answer: D
The open trade equity is calculated based on the initial margin deposit plus or minus any unrealized profit or loss on the open contract position. There is no debit balance or
or Ioan so no interest or carrying charges apply to the futures contract position.

lines: AS it relates to the margin requirement for wheat contracts for speculators and hedgers, which of the following is correct?
A. Long and short hedgers are subject to the same margin requirements as speculators
B. Long and short hedgers are subject to lower margin requirements than speculators
C. Long and short hedgers are subject to higher margin requirements than speculators
D. Long and short hedgers are not subject the margin requirements if they deposit an escrow receipt for the cash commodity, while speculators must make the required deposit
Long and short hedgers are subject to lower margin requirements than speculators as the risk is significantly reduced since they generally sell or consume the underlying commodity.
text:
QUESTION ID 25499 : series3 speculation & Hedging (WOO')
AS it relates to the margin requirement for wheat contracts for speculators and hedgers, which of the following is correct?
A. Long and short hedgers are subject to the same margin requirements as speculators
B. Long and short hedgers are subject to lower margin requirements than speculators
C. Long and short hedgers are subject to higher margin requirements than speculators
D. Long and short hedgers are not subject the margin requirements if they deposit an escrow receipt for the cash commodity, while speculators must make the required deposit
correct Answer: B
your Answer: A
Long and short hedgers are subject to lower margin requirements than speculators as the risk is significantly reduced since they generally sell or consume the underlying commodity.

someone who is contractually obligated to deliver the cash commodity but who does not own the cash commodity is considered to be short the basis and would be a long hedger.

lines: A crude oil trader purchased 5 December oil contracts at 89. Each crude contract covers 1,000 barrels of oil. The OTE in his account when the contracts are marked to the
market at 92.20 at the close of the day is $34,700

Each crude oil contract represents 1,000 barrels of oil as a result the profit per contract is $3,200 per contract ($3.20 per barrel X 1,000 barrels ) . The trader purchased 5 contracts for a total profit of $16,000 ($3,200

per contract X 5 contracts). As this appreciation has not been realized by offsetting the position OTE is credited $16,000. The total equity in the account would be $34,700 not the OTE which is the unrealized profit or

A crude oil trader purchased 5 December oil contracts at 89. Each crude contract covers 1,000 barrels of oil. The OTE in his account when the contracts are marked to the
market at 92.20 at the close of the day is $34,700

Each crude oil contract represents 1,000 barrels of oil as a result the profit per contract is $3,200 per contract ($3.20 per barrel X 1,000 barrels ) . The trader purchased 5 contracts for a total profit of $16,000 ($3,200
per contract X 5 contracts). As this appreciation has not been realized by offsetting the position OTE is credited $16,000. The total equity in the account would be $34,700 not the OTE which is the unrealized profit or
loss.

lines: Commission charges for executing a customer's order for wheat will be based on:
A. the number of bushels bought or sold
B. the price of the wheat contract
A merchant charges a commission to execute customer orders based on a round turn. A round turn consists of the opening and offsetting transactions for the futures contract.
text:
QUESTION ID 18899 : series 3- Trading Futures
Commission charges for executing a customer's order for wheat will be based on:
A. the number of bushels bought or sold
B. the price of the wheat contract
C. the time to delivery
D. a round turn
correct Answer: D
your Answer: A
A merchant charges a commission to execute customer orders based on a round turn. A round turn consists of the opening and offsetting transactions for the futures contract.

lines: A crude oil trader has noticed that tight refining capacity has caused RBOB to trade at a very high price relative to crude. He thinks that if RBOB trades any higher relative
to crude that there is a significant opportunity to profit by purchasing crude. What type of order would he most likely place?
ln the case described the trader would want to place a basis order to purchase crude if the spread between the price of the RBOB contract and crude oil contract reached a
text:
QUESTION ID 18950 : series 3 - Trading Futures
A crude oil trader has noticed that tight refining capacity has caused RBOB to trade at a very high price relative to crude. He thinks that if RBOB trades any higher relative
to crude that there is a significant opportunity to profit by purchasing crude. What type of order would he most likely place?
A. Buy stop order
B. Buy limit order
C. Basis order
D. Spread order
» correct Answer: C
your Answer: A
ln the case described the trader would want to place a basis order to purchase crude if the spread between the price of the RBOB contract and crude oil contract reached a
certain level.

lines: The visible supply for grains includes:
l. Grains that are in storage elevators
Il. Grains that are in transit
IV. Grain that remain on the farm

The visible supply report for grains includes grains that are in storage elevators, are in transit or on loading docks. The amount of grain that remains on the farm is not included in the visible supply
text:
QUESTION ID 20979 : series3 Price Forecasting
The visible supply for grains includes:
l. Grains that are in storage elevators
Il. Grains that are in transit
Ill. Grains on loading docks
IV. Grain that remain on the farm
A. I and Il
B. Ill and IV
C. l, Il and Ill
D. l, Il, Ill, IV
correct Answer: C
your Answer: D
The visible supply report for grains includes grains that are in storage elevators, are in transit or on loading docks. The amount of grain that remains on the farm is not included in the visible supply

lines: The higher the open interest is in a contract the higher:
The higher the open interest is in a contract the higher the liquidity will be in that contract. This is because there are more parties interested in trading the contract.
text:
QUESTION ID : series3 Price Forecasting
The higher the open interest is in a contract the higher:
A. the price
B. the liquidity
C. the margin requirement
D. the volatility
correct Answer: B
your Answer: D
The higher the open interest is in a contract the higher the liquidity will be in that contract. This is because there are more parties interested in trading the contract.
